responseStatus.informationUnavailableMessage: The information that usually appears here is currently unavailable.

choosePaymentOptionHeader.pageHeaderIntroduction: Choose the form of payment you want to receive on 2018-07-01. You can change your form of payment up to the last business day before your benefits begin. The forms of payment are based on other retirement choices you've made:
pageHeaderBulletList: Your last day of employment will be 2018-06-30.
You'll begin receiving the payment you choose on 2018-07-01.
No beneficiary chosen for this payment option.
choosePaymentOptionHeader.pageHeaderLegalNote: Note: The Alight Company reserves the right to correct any errors. If it's determined at any time that the information provided on this site conflicts with the benefit defined by the Employee Pension and Retirement Plan, the Employee Pension and Retirement Plan will prevail. Under the law, a plan must be operated in accordance with its terms.
choosePaymentOptionHeader.pageHeaderReviewPaymentOptions: Review the details of all your payment options. If you have any questions, specialists are ready to help at 1-888-123-4567 (8 a.m. to 4:30 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday).

paymentTableDeferSecton.deferPanelHeader: You can skip (defer) making a pension choice and come back to make that choice later.
paymentTableDeferSecton.deferPanelChooseText: You may choose to defer payment of a pension benefit if:
deferBulletList: You want to make your health care choices and prefer to begin pension benefits later.
You want to review your savings options and prefer to begin pension benefits later.
You (or your spouse, if applicable) have other sources of income and don't need your pension benefits now.
You don't want to pay taxes (if applicable) on your benefits until later.
paymentTableDeferSecton.deferModalHeader: Do you want to defer your payment?
paymentTableDeferSecton.deferModalBody: If you want to begin to receive your pension benefit at a later date, choose Yes.

primaryOptionalFormPaymentList.optionalFormPanelLeadinText: A monthly payment to you until you die (which increases, if your beneficiary dies before you), then 50 1/3% of your monthly payment to your beneficiary until your beneficiary dies
primaryOptionalFormPaymentList.optionalFormPanelText:
• After you die, 50 1/3% benefit payment to your beneficiary until he or she dies
  • If married and beneficiary is not your spouse, your spouse must consent
  • If beneficiary dies before you, the amount paid to you increases
primaryOptionalFormPaymentList.optionalFormPanelLearnMoreLink: Learn More

optionalFormToYouAmountList.popUpAmountText: If your beneficiary dies before you, your payments will increase to $1,234.00.

optionalFormBeneficiaryList.beneficiaryText: Beneficiary Benefit (for payments after your death)

primaryOptionalFormPaymentList.optionalFormPanelLeadinText: A one-time payment to you only
primaryOptionalFormPaymentList.optionalFormPanelText:
• No payment to your beneficiary
  • If married, your spouse must consent
  • Entire value of your pension benefit (or, in some cases, a portion of the entire value)
  • Subject to taxation, and the Internal Revenue Service requires 20% be automatically withheld
  • May be subject to 10% penalty tax on early distributions

primaryOptionalFormPaymentList.optionalFormPanelLeadinText: A monthly payment to you until you die, then your monthly payment to your beneficiary until the cutoff date (December 1, 2025)
primaryOptionalFormPaymentList.optionalFormPanelText:
• If you die before 10-year cutoff date, your monthly payments will continue to your beneficiary until the 10-year cutoff date
  • If you die after 10-year cutoff date, no payment to your beneficiary
  • If married, your spouse must consent
  • Beneficiary can be added after you retire
  • Contingent beneficiary can be named (in case your beneficiary dies before the 10-year cutoff date)
  • If no beneficiary when you die, remaining payments made per plan rules

hypotheticalOptionalFormPanel.hypotheticalOptionalFormPanelOverviewTex: Hypothetical payment options are available to you but you can't choose them now. We don't have all the information needed to calculate the actual amounts.
hypotheticalOptionalFormPanel.hypotheticalOptionalFormPanelSocialSecur: To calculate hypothetical options that include a Social Security level income feature, we used an estimated Social Security benefit amount. To choose one of these options, contact the Premier Benefits Center at 1-800-XXX-XXX. We'll tell you how to send us information so we can recalculate your payment options.

relativeValueParagraphList: Retirement4X is legally required to provide you with relative value information for the payment options available to you. This information will help you compare the values of your payment options, relative to each other.

It's difficult to compare all your options because they may be paid over different periods of time. For example, some are paid monthly for only your lifetime and some are paid monthly for the lifetime of you and your spouse.

While you must choose an option based on your personal situation, the relative value information shows how the value of each payment option compares to the values of the others. The relative value information is only an estimate because the true relative value depends on how long you live.

The payment option amounts are all about equal in relative value to the HARDCODED Normal OpFm. This relative value comparison is made by comparing the value of each available payment option to the value of the HARDCODED Normal OpFm. This comparison uses interest and average life expectancy assumptions.

An interest rate assumption of 10.0 was used for all other optional forms of payment. The actual relative value of payments made to you will depend on how long you live. If you want more information on the average life expectancies used in this calculation, contact the Premier Benefits Center at 1-800-XXX-XXX.

For the relative values comparison, we calculated your HARDCODED Normal OpFm using your spouse as a beneficiary and using an estimated birth date of 1970-01-01 for your spouse. You can provide the actual birth date for your spouse and receive an updated calculation. However, this request would require a recalculation of your payment options, which could change your benefit amount and may delay your payment.

tipContentList: You may be required to pay Social Security and Medicare taxes on the nonqualified pension payments that are shown on this site. These taxes, which are required by the Internal Revenue Service, are often called FICA (Federal Insurance Contributions Act) taxes. If FICA taxes apply to you, they'll be deducted from your active payroll, if applicable, or your payment. The timing of the withholding may vary.
In addition to FICA taxes, federal and state tax withholding may also apply to your nonqualified pension payment.

Your cash balance benefit has been calculated using current interest rates. Your actual cash balance benefit will be converted to the annuity options based on the interest rate in effect on your payment start date. If the interest rate at the time your payment begins is different from the current rate, your annuity options may be higher or lower than the amounts shown on this site.
Your cash balance benefit has been calculated based on:
tipContentBulletList: The value of your account from this plan
Any benefit you accrued from a previous plan

tipContentList: According to our records, a portion of your benefit has been assigned to an alternate payee due to a Qualified Domestic Relations Order (QDRO). The benefit shown has been adjusted to reflect this QDRO.

quitModalContent.title: What do you want to do?

bodyList.heading: Save
bodyList.paragraph: Keep the choices you've made, so far. Finish making pension choices later.

bodyList.heading: Quit
bodyList.paragraph: Cancel your pension request and lose all the pension choices you've made, so far.

bodyList.heading: Return
bodyList.paragraph: Go back to the current page without quitting.

editList.editMessage: You must choose a form of payment before continuing.
